title: Indra Sawhney v. Union of India (Mandal Commission Case)
summary: Upheld 27% OBC reservation recommended by Mandal Commission but imposed 50% cap on total reservations and excluded 'creamy layer' (economically advanced OBCs) from benefits. Struck down 10% reservation for economically backward among forward castes.
key_holdings: Total reservation cannot exceed 50% (except in extraordinary circumstances)
Creamy layer among OBCs must be excluded from reservation benefits
Reservation in promotions not permitted (Article 16(4A) added later)
Economically backward among forward castes reservation struck down
why_important: Definitive judgment on OBC reservation; set 50% ceiling and creamy layer doctrine still governing reservation law today.
relevance_to_legal_finance_system: OBC reservation impacts government procurement policies and public sector employment compliance.
